---
title: "Multivariate-EMS-Code"
author: "Sanjana Bajaj"
date: "2026-04-16"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```


```{r}

# check which columns have NAs or empty cells
sapply(emergency_response_data, function(col) {
  sum(is.na(col) | (is.character(col) & col == ""))
})

```

```{r}

# drop columns that are redundant or irrelevant for response time
emergency_response_data <- emergency_response_data %>%
  select(
    -Box,
    -Address,
    -`Unit ID`,
    -`Call Number`,
    -`Original Priority`,
    -`Zipcode of Incident`,
    -`Available DtTm`,
    -`Transport DtTm`,
    -`Hospital DtTm`,
    -`Watch Date`,
    -`Response DtTm`,
    -`Supervisor District`,
    -`Unit sequence in call dispatch`,
    -`Final Priority`,
    -`Station Area`,
    -`Battalion`,
    -`Call Type Group`,
  )

```

```{r}
emergency_response_data <- emergency_response_data %>% select(-`Entry DtTm`)

```

```{r}
emergency_response_data[emergency_response_data == ""] <- NA

# drop all the rows with NAs/empty cells for necessary features
emergency_response_data <- emergency_response_data %>%
  filter(
    !is.na(case_location),                     
    !is.na(`On Scene DtTm`),
    !is.na(City)
  )
```

```{r}
# some SF neighborhoods were marked in the "city" col. these should say SF since they are part of SF

emergency_response_data$City[emergency_response_data$City %in% c("Presidio", "Treasure Isla", "Hunters Point", "Fort Mason", "Yerba Buena")] <- "San Francisco"


```

```{r}

sapply(emergency_response_data, function(col) {
  sum(is.na(col) | (is.character(col) & col == ""))
})

```

```{r}
library(lubridate)
library(dplyr)

# convert to date-time
emergency_response_data <- emergency_response_data %>%
  mutate(
    `Received DtTm`  = ymd_hms(`Received DtTm`),
    `On Scene DtTm`  = ymd_hms(`On Scene DtTm`),
    `Dispatch DtTm` = ymd_hms(`Dispatch DtTm`)
  )

# create the response_time variable
emergency_response_data <- emergency_response_data %>%
  mutate(
    response_time = as.numeric(difftime(`On Scene DtTm`, `Received DtTm`, units = "mins"))  )

```

```{r}

# remove response & dispatch times < 0, and the one incident with 1911 min response & dispatch time

emergency_response_data <- emergency_response_data %>%
  filter(response_time > 0)


```

```{r}
head(sort(emergency_response_data$response_time, decreasing = TRUE), 30)

# drop the rows with response time > 180 mins -- this is very likely to be a special case, or a data entry error

emergency_response_data <- emergency_response_data %>%
  filter(response_time <= 180)

```

```{r}

hist(emergency_response_data$response_time)
```

```{r}


emergency_response_data <- emergency_response_data %>%
  mutate(
    hour = hour(`Received DtTm`),
    day_of_week = wday(`Received DtTm`, label = TRUE)
  )

```
